models.portfolioSummary: Aggregate statistics across all JCA 1221 projects, displayed as animated counters at the top of the listing page.
models.projectCard: A project shown in the filterable grid. Each card has enough info for quick scanning — photo, location, status, one key metric — and links to the detail view.
models.projectDetail: Full project detail for the one-pager view: hero image, description, key stats, technology tags, environmental impact metrics, awards, and partners.
models.projectStat: A single numeric or labeled metric for the project (e.g., daily capacity, water treated per year).
models.projectAward: An award or recognition earned by the project.
models.projectPartner: A government unit, investor, or organization that collaborated on the project.

relationships: portfolioSummary aggregates data from all projectDetail records.
Each projectCard links to one projectDetail.
Each projectDetail has many projectStats, many projectAwards, and many projectPartners.
projectAward and projectPartner are embedded within projectDetail — no standalone lists.

projects.name: Puerto Princesa Water Reclamation & Learning Center
projects.heroDescription: The Philippines' first large-scale combined septage and sewage treatment facility — a model for how environmental infrastructure can restore coastal ecosystems while remaining financially sustainable.
projects.shortDescription: City-scale biological treatment facility preventing 4,000 cubic meters of untreated wastewater from entering Puerto Princesa Bay daily.

technology.description: The facility uses a Sequential Batch Reactor (SBR) system with biological nutrient removal — the same nitrogen cycle found in healthy aquatic ecosystems. Wastewater passes through primary screening, biological treatment, secondary clarification, and UV disinfection before being released as clean recycled water. Solar-assisted operations reduce energy costs, and IoT sensors provide real-time remote monitoring of water quality parameters.

impactMetrics.label: Wastewater treated daily
impactMetrics.improvement: Prevents equivalent volume from entering the bay

impactMetrics.label: Recycled water released
impactMetrics.improvement: Dilutes remaining outflows, restoring marine habitats

impactMetrics.label: Bay water quality
impactMetrics.improvement: Now hosts Ironman triathlons and dragon boat races

impactMetrics.label: Local employment
impactMetrics.improvement: Operators, technicians, and educators from the community

awards.title: Water Reclamation Project of the Year
awards.description: Recognized for restoring coastal ecosystems through innovative, scalable treatment technology — the first Philippine facility to win this category.

partners.name: Department of Environment and Natural Resources

projects.name: Gingoog City Septage Treatment Facility
projects.heroDescription: Bringing modern wastewater treatment to Mindanao's coastal communities — a modular, scalable facility designed for Gingoog's growing population and unique geography.
projects.shortDescription: Modular septage treatment plant designed for Mindanao's coastal terrain, preventing untreated septage from reaching Macajalar Bay.

technology.description: Gingoog's facility uses a modular approach — prefabricated treatment units that can be deployed in phases as the city grows. The system combines SBR biological treatment with constructed reed bed filtration, a nature-based polishing step that removes residual nutrients before discharge. This hybrid design reduces energy consumption by 30% compared to fully mechanical systems, while the modular architecture allows rapid deployment to remote coastal terrain.

impactMetrics.label: Design treatment capacity
impactMetrics.improvement: Sufficient for Gingoog's current and near-term population

impactMetrics.label: Energy reduction
impactMetrics.improvement: Compared to fully mechanical treatment plants

impactMetrics.label: Modular expansion capability
impactMetrics.improvement: Scales with city growth without service interruption

impactMetrics.label: Local employment (est.)
impactMetrics.improvement: Construction and operations workforce from Gingoog

partners.name: Department of Public Works and Highways

projects.name: Del Carmen Solid Waste Management & Pyrolysis Facility
projects.location: Del Carmen, Siargao Island, Surigao del Norte
projects.heroDescription: JCA 1221's first solid waste management project — introducing pyrolysis technology to Siargao, converting municipal waste into biochar and clean energy while preserving the island's world-famous ecosystem.
projects.shortDescription: Pyrolysis-based solid waste management plant converting municipal waste into biochar and syngas — the first of its kind on Siargao Island.

technology.description: The Del Carmen facility marks JCA 1221's expansion from water into solid waste. The pyrolysis plant uses thermal decomposition in an oxygen-free environment to convert municipal solid waste into three products: biochar (a soil amendment that sequesters carbon), syngas (used to power the facility itself), and recovered materials. Unlike incineration, pyrolysis produces no toxic emissions. The biochar can be used by local farmers to improve soil fertility, creating a circular economy loop — waste from the community becomes a resource for the community.

impactMetrics.label: Waste diversion
impactMetrics.improvement: Diverted from landfill and open dumping on Siargao

impactMetrics.label: Biochar production
impactMetrics.improvement: Available to local farmers as carbon-sequestering soil amendment

impactMetrics.label: Energy self-sufficiency
impactMetrics.improvement: Facility powered by its own syngas output

partners.name: Department of Environment and Natural Resources
